data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Google minimizes choices to reduce decision time, optimizing user experience.
data.technical_spec: Uses simple, clean designs with essential features prominently displayed.

data.title: Fitts's Law
data.description: UI elements are positioned to enhance usability according to their expected frequency.
data.technical_spec: Quick access to the search bar and common features through strategic placement.

data.title: Law of Proximity
data.description: Related items are grouped together for easier user navigation and understanding.
data.technical_spec: Search results and related suggestions appear in logically clustered units.

data.title: Miller's Law
data.description: Information is chunked into digestible amounts to improve user comprehension.
data.technical_spec: Search features and options are segmented into intuitive, manageable sections.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Google leverages familiar design patterns to meet user expectations.
data.technical_spec: Incorporates widely-used design elements, ensuring user familiarity.
